title: Parameter --driver not support with build and buildx
body:
### Issue Description

I am using the Maven-Plugin `docker-maven-plugin` to build Containers. When using the option `buildx` for Multi-Arch-Builds, the build fails with the following message:

```
[INFO] DOCKER> [pluto:2.5-SNAPSHOT]: Created docker-build.tar in 570 milliseconds
[INFO] DOCKER> docker --config /home/.../2.5-SNAPSHOT/docker buildx create --driver docker-container --name maven
[INFO] DOCKER> Error: unknown flag: --driver
[INFO] DOCKER> See 'podman buildx --help'
```

As far as I understand the situation, podman does not support the parameter `--driver`. Would it be possible to support this for a better compatibility? [Here](https://docs.docker.com/build/drivers/) is the respective documentation from Docker.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a basic Maven Project using `docker-maven-plugin`.
2. Configure `buildx` for the image.
3. Run `mvn clean package`.


### Describe the results you received

The build is aborted with the following error message:

```
[INFO] DOCKER> [pluto:2.5-SNAPSHOT]: Created docker-build.tar in 570 milliseconds
[INFO] DOCKER> docker --config /home/.../2.5-SNAPSHOT/docker buildx create --driver docker-container --name maven
[INFO] DOCKER> Error: unknown flag: --driver
[INFO] DOCKER> See 'podman buildx --help'
```

### Describe the results you expected

The build should not abort.

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

### Additional environment details

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: I don't think only `--driver` is the problem `podman buildx` does not also supports `create` since podman has no such feature of builder instances.
This maybe falls into a new feature not a bug.
I agree this is a feature and not even sure how we would implement it.
Thanks for your comments. I will just leave this feature request here in case other stumble over it, too.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
